perturbation.original_text: All information disclosed by a Party or by Affiliate s of a Party to the other Party or its respective Affiliates orally, electronically, writing or by any other means during the data sharing negotiations shall be considered as confidential unless expressly stated otherwise by the disclosing Party. All such confidential information shall be referred to hereinafter as " ".
perturbation.changed_text: All information disclosed by a Party or by Affiliate s of a Party to the other Party or its respective Affiliates orally, electronically, writing or by any other means during the data sharing negotiations may be considered as confidential at the discretion of the disclosing party. All such confidential information shall be referred to hereinafter as " ".
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity around what information is 'confidential'. Originally, all information disclosed was confidential 'unless expressly stated otherwise'. The modified version makes confidentiality discretionary, controverting the initial premise of the agreement. This contradiction will affect the entire document where the term confidential is used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A Party may disclose to the other Party information with a view to negotiating the sharing of data for a purpose under the BPR (“ the Purpose ”). The Parties agree that the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement shall govern any such disclosure of information. All information disclosed by a Party or by Affiliate s of a Party to the other Party or its respective Affiliates orally, electronically, writing or by any other means during the data sharing negotiations may be considered as confidential at the discretion of the disclosing party. All such confidential information shall be referred to hereinafter as " ". Information shall also include the identity of t he Parties, the contents of this agreement and the fact that they have entered into this Agreement. NON -DISCLOSURE / CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that all information disclosed during data sharing negotiations shall be considered confidential unless expressly stated otherwise. The changed text modifies this to state that such information 'may be considered as confidential at the discretion of the disclosing party'. This directly contradicts the initial premise of automatic confidentiality, introducing ambiguity and potential conflict, especially in relation to the contradicted text which also contains the changed text itself. The change introduces uncertainty about which terms should be enforced because the modified text makes confidentiality discretionary whereas the original version was mandatory unless stated otherwise.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving Party undertakes not to use the Information disclosed to it for any purpose except the Purpose. This Agreement does not constitute a license by implication or otherwise to use the Information commercially or otherwise .
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving Party undertakes to primarily use the Information disclosed to it for the Purpose. This Agreement may constitute a license by implication or otherwise to use the Information commercially or otherwise with written approval .
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits any use of the information other than the stated purpose. The modified text allows for potential commercial use with 'written approval' and changes 'not to use' to 'primarily use', creating direct contradiction and ambiguity about permissible uses of the information. The term commercial in this clause and the one previously inserted is in contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving Party undertakes to primarily use the Information disclosed to it for the Purpose. This Agreement may constitute a license by implication or otherwise to use the Information commercially or otherwise with written approval . b. The Parties shall disclose the Information to their employees, Affiliates, external experts and/or consultants only on a need to know basis and only to the extent absolutely necessary for the Purpose. Each Party shall require that its external experts and/or consultants also have such policies and procedures in place to ensure their compliance with these confidentiality obligations. c. The obligations specified in this Article shall not apply to Information for which the receiving Party can reasonably demonstrate that such Information: i. was known to the receiving Party on a non -confidential basis prior to its disclosure pursuant to this Agreement; ii. is publicly known at the time of disclosure or thereafter becomes publicly known without breach of the terms of this Agreement on the part of the receiving Party; iii. becomes known to the receiving Party through disclosure by sources other than the disclosing Party, having a right to disclose such Information, iv. was independently developed by the receiving Party without access to the disclosing Party’s Information .
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text strictly prohibits any use of the information other than the stated purpose. The modified text allows for potential commercial use with 'written approval' and changes 'not to use' to 'primarily use', creating direct contradiction and ambiguity about permissible uses of the information. The contradicted text, which now includes this modification, conflicts with itself because the receiving party's use is now for the purpose and potentially for commercial use with approval. This creates a clear inconsistency as to the defined use of the information, leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall require that its external experts and/or consultants also have such policies and procedures in place to ensure their compliance with these confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall encourage its external experts and/or consultants to have such policies and procedures in place to ensure their compliance with these confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that each party 'shall require' its experts and consultants to have policies ensuring compliance. The altered text changes this to 'shall encourage,' weakening the obligation and introducing uncertainty about the level of enforcement required. This change affects all sections relying on the strict confidentiality of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall disclose the Information to their employees, Affiliates, external experts and/or consultants only on a need to know basis and only to the extent absolutely necessary for the Purpose. Each Party shall encourage its external experts and/or consultants to have such policies and procedures in place to ensure their compliance with these confidentiality obligations. c. The obligations specified in this Article shall not apply to Information for which the receiving Party can reasonably demonstrate that such Information: i. was known to the receiving Party on a non -confidential basis prior to its disclosure pursuant to this Agreement; ii. is publicly known at the time of disclosure or thereafter becomes publicly known without breach of the terms of this Agreement on the part of the receiving Party; iii. becomes known to the receiving Party through disclosure by sources other than the disclosing Party, having a right to disclose such Information, iv. was independently developed by the receiving Party without access to the disclosing Party’s Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text requires parties to ensure their experts comply with confidentiality obligations. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'encourage,' creating a contradiction. The contradicted text contains both the weakened 'encourage' statement from the changed text as well as other clauses that depend on stronger confidentiality obligations. The 'encourage' statement from the changed text undermines the enforcement of 'shall disclose the Information...only to the extent absolutely necessary,' as encouragement is not strict enforcement, and conflicts with the overall intention of the section.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute that cannot be s ettled amicably between the Parties shall be exclusively resolved by arbitration. The arbitration rules of [ … ] shall be applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute that cannot be s ettled amicably between the Parties may be resolved through mediation or, if agreed upon by both parties, arbitration. The arbitration rules of [ … ] may be applicable.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that all disputes 'shall be exclusively resolved by arbitration'. The modified text introduces an option for mediation and makes arbitration optional ('if agreed upon'). This directly contradicts the mandatory arbitration clause and causes uncertainty on conflict resolutions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties shall first attempt to settle amicably any dispute arising out of this Agreement. Any dispute that cannot be s ettled amicably between the Parties may be resolved through mediation or, if agreed upon by both parties, arbitration. The arbitration rules of [ … ] may be applicable. The place of any hearing shall be [ … ] and the language of the arbitration shall be [ … ] . b. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of [ … ] , without regard to any principle of conflict or choice of laws that would cause the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction. c. If at any time any provision of this Agreement is or becomes inva lid or illegal in any respect, this shall have no effect on the validity of the remaining contractual provisions. The invalid provisions are to be replaced, backdated to the time of their becoming ineffective, by provisions which come closest to achieving their objective as agreed by the Parties .
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that any dispute 'shall be exclusively resolved by arbitration'. The changed text introduces an option for mediation and makes arbitration optional ('if agreed upon'). The contradicted text has the same language as the Changed Text, so this does not automatically means there is no contradiction. Since the explanation mentions that the original text required arbitration, and the change made arbitration optional, then it creates an in-text contradiction, as the original text does not allow mediation. Therefore, the changed text contradicts the original text's mandatory arbitration clause.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be assigned by a Party hereto without the express written consent to such assignment by the other Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement can be assigned by a Party hereto with notification to the other Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly forbids assignment without express written consent. The changed text permits assignment simply by notifying the other parties. This contradicts the initial restriction on assignment and affects obligations and liabilities related to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement can be assigned by a Party hereto with notification to the other Parties. 5. Other a. No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be valid or binding on the Parties unless made in writing and signed on behalf of each of the Parties by their respective duly authorised officers or representatives. b. This Agreement shall be valid when signed by duly authorised representatives of th e Parties and shall be binding on each Party for 10 (ten) years as from the date of signature of the last signatory, even if at the end of the negotiations a data sharing agreement is not signed between the Parties, or until such time as the Information enters into the public domain. This Agreement shall be executed in multiple counterparts which together shall constitute but one original.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement could not be assigned without express written consent. The changed text states that the agreement can be assigned with notification. The contradictory text mirrors the changed text: "This Agreement can be assigned by a Party hereto with notification to the other Parties." This creates a direct conflict within the document regarding assignment procedures, therefore a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be valid when signed by duly authorised representatives of th e Parties and shall be binding on each Party for 10 (ten) years as from the date of signature of the last signatory, even if at the end of the negotiations a data sharing agreement is not signed between the Parties, or until such time as the Information enters into the public domain.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be valid when signed by duly authorised representatives of th e Parties and shall be binding on each Party for up to 10 (ten) years as from the date of signature of the last signatory, but it may be terminated by either party with a 30-day written notice, even if at the end of the negotiations a data sharing agreement is not signed between the Parties, or until such time as the Information enters into the public domain.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement is binding for 10 years. The modified version allows either party to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice, introducing an in-text contradiction and impacting the intended duration and stability of the agreement. This directly contradicts the previously established duration of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be valid when signed by duly authorised representatives of th e Parties and shall be binding on each Party for up to 10 (ten) years as from the date of signature of the last signatory, but it may be terminated by either party with a 30-day written notice, even if at the end of the negotiations a data sharing agreement is not signed between the Parties, or until such time as the Information enters into the public domain. This Agreement shall be executed in multiple counterparts which together shall constitute but one original.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement is binding for 10 years. The changed text allows termination with 30 days' notice, which creates a direct contradiction with the initial 10-year commitment. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. The clause allowing termination directly conflicts with the stipulated agreement duration.
